title: Whoop fires back at Google's Fitbit with in-app doctor consultations and deeper medical tracking
snippet: WHOOP is set to launch new health features, including in-app video consultations with clinicians and EHR syncing for improved medical history access.

title: iOS 26.5 likely to arrive this week with these 3 new features: Here's what you should know
snippet: Apple released a release candidate (RC) for iOS 26.5, expected to launch this week. The update will include features like RCS messages with encryption, Suggested Places in Apple Maps, and new Pride Edition wallpapers.

title: You can now control Google Chrome with OpenAI's Codex: Here's how to set it up
snippet: OpenAI has launched a new Codex extension for Chrome allows users to control the browser via the AI assistant. Codex can be used to accessing tasks like emails and updating records.&nbsp;

title: Meta quietly removes end-to-end encryption from Instagram: What does it mean for your private DMs?
snippet: Instagram has discontinued end-to-end encryption on the platform, meaning user messages are now only protected by standard encryption.

OpenAI lets employees cash out up to $30 million each in share sale, valuation hits $400 billion: Report
snippet: OpenAI employees, both current and former, collectively sold $6.6 billion in shares during a secondary transaction in October 2025. This sale valued the artificial intelligence company at an estimated $400 billion, allowing approximately 75 participants to liquidate up to $30 million each in equity.
